item.village-quest.kupfer_groschen.lore: Leftover from the old mint. Not part of the current purse.
item.village-quest.eisen_groschen.lore: Silvermark: The common coin of the road.
item.village-quest.magic_shard.lore: 1 shard reveals an extra daily. 10 shards awaken a hidden trial at the Questmaster.
item.village-quest.starreach_ring.lore: Carry in your offhand to place and reach blocks 2 steps farther away.
item.village-quest.merchant_seal.lore: Sneak-use on a pilgrim or wandering trader once per day to refresh that merchant's wares. On pilgrims, it may also change the current road rumor.
item.village-quest.shepherd_flute.lore: Its call draws nearby animals toward you for a short while.
item.village-quest.starreach_map.lore: A red X marks the buried cache that guards the ring.
text.village-quest.daily.shard.title: The Questmaster's Call
text.village-quest.daily.shard.body.1: Today's duty is done. Yet with old magic I could still reveal one more task for this day.
text.village-quest.daily.shard.body.2: If you entrust me with a Magic Shard, I will uncover an extra quest. The shard will be consumed.
text.village-quest.daily.shard.accept: 1. I will use a Magic Shard
text.village-quest.special.offer.accept: 1. I will bind the ten shards
message.village-quest.questmaster.comple: You already completed this quest today. Come back tomorrow!
You already fulfilled even the extra task today. Come back tomorrow!
message.village-quest.questmaster.busy: The Questmaster is already speaking with someone else.
message.village-quest.questmaster.too_fa: You are too far away from the Questmaster.
message.village-quest.questmaster.defend: The Questmaster has no patience for questions right now.
message.village-quest.questmaster.warnin: This is a quest board, not a brawling pit.
One more hit and I will revise your standing personally.
You are mistaking discipline for weakness.
Careful. I keep records and grudges equally well.
message.village-quest.questmaster.retali: Very well. Practical instruction it is.
You asked for correction. Here it comes.
Let us mark this as field training.
message.village-quest.questmaster.death_: You killed the Questmaster. Your call will go unanswered for %s.
message.village-quest.questmaster.shard_: You no longer have a Magic Shard with you.
The Questmaster could not find another fitting task.
message.village-quest.questmaster.specia: You no longer carry the ten Magic Shards needed for this hidden trial.
The shards hum in unison. Return to the Questmaster and claim the hidden map.
The Questmaster could not bind a hidden cache for you yet. Try again in a moment.
The buried cache does not answer your hand.
Do not break the cache itself. Unearth it and open it instead.
command.village-quest.questadmin.player_: A player is required for this command.
The Questmaster could not find a place to appear.
The Questmaster ignores your call for %s.
command.village-quest.questadmin.resetda: Your daily quest status for today has been reset.
Daily quest status for %s has been reset.
An admin reset your daily quest status for today.
command.village-quest.questadmin.nextdai: Next daily test prepared. Previous quest: %s
Next daily test prepared for %s. Previous quest: %s
An admin prepared your daily status for a next-day test.
command.village-quest.questadmin.complet: There is no active daily quest to complete immediately.
Active daily quest for %s completed immediately: %s
An admin completed your active daily quest immediately: %s
command.village-quest.questadmin.shardca: Your hidden shard quest was moved directly to the map and cache phase.
The hidden shard quest of %s was moved directly to the map and cache phase.
Your hidden shard quest was moved directly to the map and cache phase by an admin.
The map and cache phase of the hidden shard quest could not be prepared right now.
You were teleported directly to the buried shard cache.
%s was teleported directly to the buried shard cache.
An admin teleported you directly to the buried shard cache.
The buried shard cache could not be resolved or found right now.
command.village-quest.questadmin.dailypr: %s has no active daily quest today.
command.village-quest.questadmin.showdai: Today's daily offer cannot be shown again because the quest is already active.
Today's daily offer cannot be shown again because the quest is already completed today.
Today's daily offer could not be shown.
Today's daily offer shown again for %s: %s
An admin showed today's daily offer to you again: %s
command.village-quest.generic.not_allowe: You can't do that right now.
command.village-quest.wallet.convert.non: No legacy coin items were found in your inventory.
An admin adjusted your wallet: %s. New balance: %s
An admin set your wallet to: %s
command.village-quest.shop.offer_missing: That shop offer does not exist.
quest.village-quest.daily.honey.title: The Queen's Gift
quest.village-quest.daily.honey.offer.1:
This year's bloom was so rich that my hives are overflowing - honey is already dripping from the combs!
If nobody helps soon, the bees will run out of room and leave.
quest.village-quest.daily.honey.offer.2:
I'm looking for someone to help harvest the honey and cut the combs,
before the whole yard turns sticky like a sweet shop.
A small purse and honest thanks shall be your pay.
quest.village-quest.daily.honey.completi: The yard smells like summer again, and the bees dance as if it had never been any other way.
You saved more than you know - thank you, friend.
I'm happy to share a few coins for the trouble.
quest.village-quest.daily.pet_collar.off: Does your dog or cat wear a collar as faded as an old market stall in the rain?
Take up dye and skill, and give your companion the adornment it deserves!
quest.village-quest.daily.pet_collar.com: The color shines, and your faithful companion wears it with pride.
Such care travels fast - thank you for your effort.
Here are a few coins in thanks for your handiwork.
quest.village-quest.daily.wheat.title: Bakehouse Help
quest.village-quest.daily.wheat.offer.1: The bakehouse is short on both grain and finished loaves, and morning will come fast.
quest.village-quest.daily.wheat.offer.2: Harvest 12 ripe wheat crops and craft 3 loaves of bread yourself so the ovens are not heated for nothing.
quest.village-quest.daily.wheat.completi: The sacks are filled, and the smell of fresh bread finally returns to the bakehouse.
A village eats well when someone sees the work through from field to oven.
Take a few coins in thanks for your effort.
quest.village-quest.daily.potato.title: Kitchen Supplies
quest.village-quest.daily.potato.offer.1: The kitchen does not need just one full basket - it needs the right mix to keep supper going.
quest.village-quest.daily.potato.offer.2: Harvest 10 ripe potato plants and 10 ripe carrot plants so the pots do not go empty tonight.
quest.village-quest.daily.potato.complet: The pantry baskets are balanced again, and the kitchen can finally work properly.
Anyone who keeps both fields moving saves a village from many thin meals.
Here, take some coins for the road.
quest.village-quest.daily.wood.title: Fuel for the Workshop
quest.village-quest.daily.wood.offer.1: The workshop is short at both ends: timber above ground, fuel below it.
quest.village-quest.daily.wood.offer.2: Chop 8 logs and mine 8 coal ore blocks so the bench and furnace can work together again.
quest.village-quest.daily.wood.completio: The woodpile is stacked, the fuel is ready, and the workshop has found its rhythm again.
Good work rarely fixes only one side of the problem - you supplied both.
Take these coins as payment for the way back.
quest.village-quest.daily.coal.title: A Favor for the Smithy
quest.village-quest.daily.coal.offer.1: The smithy needs ore for metal and coal for heat - one without the other is only half a day's work.
quest.village-quest.daily.coal.offer.2: Bring back 6 raw iron and 8 coal from your own mining so the hammer and furnace do not fall silent today.
quest.village-quest.daily.coal.completio: The furnace is glowing, the ore is ready, and honest work rings through the smithy again.
Anyone who brings both fire and metal helps more than words ever could.
Take these coins as your pay.
quest.village-quest.daily.wool.title: Wool for the Weaver
quest.village-quest.daily.wool.offer.1: The weaver has empty hands again: no fresh cut from the pasture, no wool for the loom.
quest.village-quest.daily.wool.offer.2: Shear 3 sheep and pick up 6 wool yourself so thread can become cloth again.
quest.village-quest.daily.wool.completio: The wool bundles are stacked, and the loom can finally start moving again.
Careful work out on the pasture saves twice the labor at the frame.
Here are coins in thanks for your trouble.
quest.village-quest.daily.river.title: Meal from the River
quest.village-quest.daily.river.offer.1: The river is full of good catch today, but without steady hands the pan will stay cold.
quest.village-quest.daily.river.offer.2: Catch 3 fish and cook 3 of them yourself so a proper fresh meal can make it onto the table.
quest.village-quest.daily.river.completi: The catch is in, and the smell of a proper riverside meal finally drifts from the kitchen.
Anyone who catches and cooks the meal themselves brings home more than food alone.
Take a few coins for your work.
quest.village-quest.daily.autumn.title: Autumn Harvest
quest.village-quest.daily.autumn.offer.1: The heavy autumn fruits are ready in the fields, but nobody has enough time to clear both rows before they spoil.
quest.village-quest.daily.autumn.offer.2: Harvest 6 pumpkins and 6 melons straight from the fields before a good harvest is left lying there.
quest.village-quest.daily.autumn.complet: The fruit is gathered in, and the pantry finally looks like a proper autumn storehouse again.
You did not merely collect produce - you saved the harvest at the right time.
Here are coins for you and your timely help.
quest.village-quest.daily.smelt.title: Smelting for the Smithy
quest.village-quest.daily.smelt.offer.1: The smithy is not short on will, only on properly mined ore and iron that has actually been smelted through.
quest.village-quest.daily.smelt.offer.2: Bring back 8 raw iron from your own mining and 8 iron ingots smelted by your own hand so hammer and anvil can work again.
quest.village-quest.daily.smelt.completi: The ore is mined, the ingots are stacked, and proper work rings through the smithy again.
Anyone who does not stop at mining but finishes the metal lifts the burden from an entire workshop.
Take coins as a sign of thanks for your effort.
quest.village-quest.daily.stall.title: New Life in the Stable
quest.village-quest.daily.stall.offer.1: The stable has gone too quiet, and without new stock even the best feed is only half-used labor.
quest.village-quest.daily.stall.offer.2: Put 2 animals into breeding mode with real breeding food so new life returns to the pens.
quest.village-quest.daily.stall.completi: There is movement in the stable again, and the promise of new life finally hangs over the pens.
Care with livestock always pays twice - today that is easy to see.
Here are coins for your help.
quest.village-quest.daily.trade.title: Business in the Village
quest.village-quest.daily.trade.offer.1: Market day never truly starts if nobody trades with the villagers and gets the goods moving again.
quest.village-quest.daily.trade.offer.2: Complete 2 real villager trades and earn at least 1 emerald in the process so village business comes back to life.
quest.village-quest.daily.trade.completi: The trade crates are changing hands again, and the village market finally feels alive.
Anyone who reconnects supply and demand helps a village more than a single full sack ever could.
Take coins for your good business.
quest.village-quest.special.shards.title: Whispers of the Shards
quest.village-quest.special.shards.offer: Ten Magic Shards answer one another in your pack. If you trust them to me, I can bind their old voices into a hidden trial of magic.
Bring me 16 Amethyst Shards, brew 3 potions, enchant 1 item, obtain 1 Ender Pearl, and obtain 1 Blaze Rod. The %s shards will be consumed.
quest.village-quest.special.shards.cache: The map's red X points toward a buried cache in the Overworld.
Dig beneath the marked ground and recover the Starreach Ring.
quest.village-quest.special.shards.map_g: Your proof is complete. I have drawn the hidden cache onto this map.
Follow the red X, dig below the marked earth, and claim the Starreach Ring for yourself.
quest.village-quest.special.shards.map_r: I have marked the hidden cache for you again. Trust the red X and dig where the earth sounds hollow.
quest.village-quest.special.shards.compl: The Starreach Ring answers your hand at last, and the old shard-song falls quiet.
Wear it in your offhand and the world itself yields two more steps to your reach.
quest.village-quest.special.merchant.off: Your name is spoken well on the market road. If you wish, I can put that standing to the proof and bind a seal fit for a true traveling broker.
Complete 5 villager trades, earn 8 emeralds from those deals, and make 1 purchase from a pilgrim stall. Do this, and the seal is yours.
quest.village-quest.special.merchant.com: The Merchant's Seal now rests in your hand, and every road-side stall should honor your standing.
Sneak-use it on a pilgrim or wandering trader once per day to refresh that merchant's wares.
quest.village-quest.special.flute.title: Shepherd's Flute
The herds know calm hands, and the village has taken notice. If you would prove that gift, I can name you to an old pastoral trial.
Breed 4 animals, shear 3 sheep, and gather 8 wool. Return when the pens are lively again, and the flute will be yours.
quest.village-quest.special.flute.comple: The Shepherd's Flute now answers your breath, and even skittish creatures listen to its call.
Play it to draw nearby animals close for a short while.
screen.village-quest.journal.title: Journal
screen.village-quest.journal.summary.sto: Every deed in this village is recorded in its archives. Whoever crosses the paths of its people writes themselves into the chronicle. This journal bears witness to you.
screen.village-quest.journal.commands.in: These pages list the commands that are actually useful for normal players during everyday quest play.
screen.village-quest.journal.commands.av: All commands shown here are player-safe. No admin permissions are needed.
screen.village-quest.journal.commands.qu: Call the Questmaster near you to offer or review quests.
screen.village-quest.journal.commands.wa: Show your current digital wallet balance.
screen.village-quest.journal.commands.re: Show your current standing in farming, crafting, animals, village trade, and roadside watch.
screen.village-quest.journal.commands.jo: Open or close this journal at any time.
screen.village-quest.journal.commands.qu: These commands matter once a quest offer or tracked quest is already in play.
screen.village-quest.journal.commands.ac: Accept the quest offer currently shown by the Questmaster.
screen.village-quest.journal.commands.qu: Toggle the permanent quest tracker in the top-right corner.
Force the permanent quest tracker to stay visible.
Hide the permanent quest tracker again.
screen.village-quest.journal.flow.1: Use /questmaster to call the Questmaster to your location.
screen.village-quest.journal.flow.2: Review the offer in the Questmaster board and accept it there, or use /dailyquest accept while a chat offer is open.
screen.village-quest.journal.flow.3: While a quest is active, progress now appears directly in the action bar. You can also enable the permanent tracker.
screen.village-quest.journal.flow.4: Normal daily quests reset after midnight. Magic Shards can unlock one extra daily and, in greater number, a hidden trial.
screen.village-quest.journal.flow.5: You can review active quest progress here at any time and cancel the current daily or weekly with the red X.
screen.village-quest.journal.flow.6: When the ledger asks for crafted or forged proof, only fresh work made during the active quest counts. Old village stock from a chest does not.
The village remembers every steady hand. Each completed task strengthens your standing with the people who live and work here.
Next unlock: %1$s at %3$s reputation, once %2$s is complete
%1$s still waits on the completion of %2$s
screen.village-quest.journal.relics.head: Relics
screen.village-quest.journal.relics.stor: These special items are only listed here while you are actually carrying them.
screen.village-quest.journal.relics.surv: Sneak-right-click to change the bearing. Right-click to follow it. Home answers every 30 minutes, but asks for a second click within 10 seconds. Later, the Questmaster can steady it against field bearings, and a pilgrim can lay old roadmarks upon it.
screen.village-quest.journal.help.1: There are daily quests that can be completed anew each day.
screen.village-quest.journal.help.2: The daily reset happens after midnight.
screen.village-quest.journal.help.3: You can click the chat message to accept a quest.
screen.village-quest.journal.help.4: In the journal you can manage quests, check progress, and cancel them.
text.village-quest.shop.title: Village Shop
text.village-quest.shop.coming_soon: No merchant is carrying wares right now. Try again later.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.proviantbe: Contains 32 golden carrots and 1-2 extra finds: ingots, emeralds, diamonds, torches, food, coal, or rarely a golden apple.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.hunters_sa: A field satchel stocked with arrows and provisions for the next hunt.
A sweet decorative piece for pantry, counter, or tavern corner.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.bienenwabe: Golden decor for every beekeeper's nook and any cozy home.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.beekeepers: A practical hive set with a campfire and bottles for the next good harvest.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.gemaelde_b: A warm keepsake for a parlor, hallway, or sleeping chamber.
text.village-quest.reputation.next_unloc: Next unlock: %1$s at %3$s reputation, once %2$s is complete
text.village-quest.reputation.story_requ: %1$s still waits on the completion of %2$s
message.village-quest.project.level_bonu: %1$s adds a level bonus of %2$s to this reward.
message.village-quest.questtracker.auto_: The quest tracker now follows your work in the top-right corner. Use %1$s to turn it on or off.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.gemaelde_m: Rare wall decor for those who can afford a truly regal accent.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.gemaelde_p: A reverent wall piece for halls with a taste for strange saints.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.gemaelde_o: A forceful pointing scene for rooms where somebody always knows better.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.gemaelde_g: A gentle classic for quiet corners and good company.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.gemaelde_s: A sideways stare for walls that should look suspicious in return.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.gemaelde_t: A bold portrait for chambers that prefer a confident presence.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.gemaelde_a: A stern face for stone halls, guild rooms, and serious spaces.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.gemaelde_h: A broad cheerful scene for bright rooms, riverbanks, and easy afternoons.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.zombie_tro: A grim keepsake for hunters who want proof on the wall.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.skeleton_t: A stark skull for halls that prefer their warnings literal.
text.village-quest.shop.offer.creeper_tr: A rare blast-marked trophy for a hunter of real nerve.
message.village-quest.pilgrim.arrived: A traveling pilgrim has made camp nearby.
message.village-quest.pilgrim.busy: The pilgrim is already bargaining with someone else.
message.village-quest.pilgrim.too_far: The pilgrim is too far away.
message.village-quest.pilgrim.defending: The pilgrim is in no mood to trade right now.
message.village-quest.pilgrim.warning.3: Try that again and the road will push back.
message.village-quest.pilgrim.warning.4: You are one hit away from the expensive response.
message.village-quest.pilgrim.retaliatio: Fine. Then I bargain with steel.
The road taught me this part personally.
message.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.r: Return to the pilgrim. %s is ready to be settled.
message.village-quest.special.merchant.r: Return to the Questmaster. The Merchant's Seal is ready to be claimed.
message.village-quest.special.merchant.u: The seal has already refreshed a merchant's wares today.
message.village-quest.special.merchant.r: The Merchant's Seal refreshes the merchant's wares.
The Merchant's Seal refreshes the pilgrim's wares and stirs a different road rumor besides.
message.village-quest.special.merchant.t: That merchant is already bargaining with someone else.
message.village-quest.project.quest_echo: The Apiary Charter makes this sort of field work count plainly in the village ledger.
Since the Forge Charter was entered, the village weighs this kind of work more carefully.
Since the Market Charter was written, honest trade and road custom are weighed together more carefully.
The Pasture Charter makes ordered care at the pens stand out at once.
Since the Watch Bell was raised, proven road watch is entered more plainly in the ledger.
message.village-quest.project.reward_ech: Because the Apiary Charter stands, this help settles deeper among the fields and hives.
Because the Forge Charter stands, this labor carries lasting weight in the hall.
Because the Market Charter stands, the pilgrim now treats this village as a place whose road signs are worth trusting.
Because the Pasture Charter stands, this care is remembered across the pens and fields.
Because the Watch Bell stands, this roadside watch leaves a stronger mark on the village.
message.village-quest.special.flute.read: Return to the Questmaster. The Shepherd's Flute is ready to be claimed.
message.village-quest.special.flute.no_a: No nearby animals answer the flute.
message.village-quest.special.flute.wool: Bring the gathered wool with you for the turn-in.
command.village-quest.questadmin.pilgrim: The pilgrim could not be spawned safely.
Pilgrim rumor testing is now forced on for you. Pilgrims can show a rumor tab even without village projects.
Pilgrim rumor testing is now forced on for %s.
An admin forced pilgrim rumor testing on for you. Pilgrims can now show a rumor tab immediately.
Pilgrim rumor testing is now cleared for you. Rumors now follow normal project unlocks again.
Pilgrim rumor testing is now cleared for %s.
An admin cleared your pilgrim rumor test override. Rumors now follow normal project unlocks again.
screen.village-quest.pilgrim.title: Pilgrim Trader
screen.village-quest.pilgrim.wallet_hint: Payment is taken directly from your wallet.
screen.village-quest.pilgrim.close_hint: Press Esc to leave the stall.
screen.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.ac: The pilgrim has trusted you with a small road contract.
screen.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.re: The contract is fulfilled and ready to be settled.
screen.village-quest.journal.active.head: Active Quests
screen.village-quest.journal.active.dail: Red X: cancel the current daily quest.
screen.village-quest.journal.active.week: Red X: cancel the current weekly quest.
screen.village-quest.journal.active.none: Tip: use the Questmaster button below to call him for new work.
text.village-quest.special.merchant.offe: 1. I will prove my standing on the road
text.village-quest.special.flute.offer.a: 1. I will take the shepherd's trial
Find a way to the island and bring back my
quest.village-quest.wolkensprung.chest_h: Don't dawdle! Go and bring my
screen.village-quest.questmaster.title: Quest Board
screen.village-quest.questmaster.daily.u: The Questmaster has not prepared a task for today yet.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.daily.c: Today's duty is already done. Perhaps a Magic Shard can pull more from the parchment.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.daily.b: An additional task is waiting for your decision.
Even today's extra task has already been completed.
No weekly assignment is available right now.
This week's assignment is already complete. Return after the weekly reset for a new contract.
A new weekly will appear after the next reset.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.special: You have already earned this relic.
Finish your active daily quest first.
The Questmaster is already guiding another relic quest.
Special Commissions
The Questmaster now keeps a careful ledger of your standing in the village.
As your reputation grows or stranger shards gather around you, rarer commissions will appear here.
The shard trial is underway. Fulfill the magical objectives and return.
The trial is complete. The Questmaster can now hand you the treasure map.
The ring is not yet recovered. Ask for the treasure map again if you need it.
Magic Shards: %s/%s
Gather %s of %s Magic Shards to begin this hidden trial.
Prove yourself upon the market road and return for your seal.
The market road is complete. Merchant's Seal is waiting for you.
Trade with villagers, earn emeralds, and buy once from a pilgrim.
Village Trade reputation: %s/%s
Aid the herd and gather wool to earn the Shepherd's Flute.
The herd trial is complete. The Shepherd's Flute can now be claimed.
Breed animals, shear sheep, and gather wool.
Animals reputation: %s/%s
Effect: When carried in the offhand it grants +2 block reach while building.
Effect: Rerolls pilgrim or wandering trader wares once per in-game day. On pilgrims, it may also reroll the current road rumor.
Effect: Calls nearby animals to you for a short while.
command.village-quest.questadmin.resetwe: Your weekly quest state was reset.
Reset the weekly quest state for %s.
Your weekly quest state was reset by an admin.
command.village-quest.questadmin.nextwee: Prepared the next weekly quest. Previous: %s
Prepared the next weekly quest for %s. Previous: %s
An admin prepared your next weekly quest.
command.village-quest.questadmin.complet: No active weekly quest could be completed.
An admin completed your weekly quest: %s
quest.village-quest.weekly.harvest.title: Harvest for the Village
quest.village-quest.weekly.harvest.offer: The village granary should not stand half-empty by week's end.
Bring in grain, roots, and enough bread to show the fields were kind.
quest.village-quest.weekly.harvest.compl: The granary is stocked and the ovens stayed warm all week.
The Questmaster marks your name beside the village stores.
Harvest for the Village is complete.
quest.village-quest.weekly.bakehouse.off: The bakehouse needs more than one rush of flour to feed the village.
Keep bread, pies, and hot potatoes ready for the week ahead.
quest.village-quest.weekly.bakehouse.com: The shelves are full and the bakehouse smells like a feast.
Even the latecomers will find something warm waiting for them.
quest.village-quest.weekly.smith.title: Smith's Week
quest.village-quest.weekly.smith.offer.1: A busy forge needs ore in the yard and ingots on the racks.
quest.village-quest.weekly.smith.offer.2: Mine the metal, fire the furnaces, and keep the smith supplied all week.
quest.village-quest.weekly.smith.complet: The forge roared bright and the smith never ran out of work.
Fresh metal now hangs where empty hooks stood before.
quest.village-quest.weekly.pasture.title: Stall and Pasture
quest.village-quest.weekly.pasture.offer: Healthy herds are the village's quiet wealth.
Breed the animals, shear the flock, and gather enough wool for the week.
quest.village-quest.weekly.pasture.compl: The barns are lively and the wool baskets finally look full.
The Questmaster notes that the herds have been well kept.
quest.village-quest.weekly.market.title: Market Week
A proper market week brings silver, emeralds, and word from the road.
Trade with villagers, earn emeralds, and do business with the pilgrim too.
quest.village-quest.weekly.market.comple: The market square stayed busy and the coin never stopped moving.
Both village stalls and the road merchant profited from your work.
quest.village-quest.weekly.nightwatch.of: The nights have grown restless around the village paths.
Thin the undead before their numbers turn bold.
quest.village-quest.weekly.nightwatch.co: The roads are quieter now and the lanterns burn with less fear.
The Questmaster pays you like someone who guarded more than a gate.
quest.village-quest.weekly.roadwarden.of: Merchants and pilgrims need safer roads than the wild has left them.
Drive back the monsters and deal with the creepers before they ruin the way.
quest.village-quest.weekly.roadwarden.co: The road signs still stand, and the path is safer for every traveler.
The Questmaster records that you kept the route open all week.
item.village-quest.apiarists_smoker.lore: Up to 10 times per day, ready a hive for harvest and calm the nearby bees.
item.village-quest.surveyors_compass.lor: Home every 30 min. Field bearings and roadmarks must be inscribed first.
text.village-quest.reputation.no_unlocks: No unlocks are tied to this reputation yet.
quest.village-quest.daily.zombie_cull.of: The dead have started wandering too close to the village lanes again.
Cut down 8 zombies before the shambling crowd grows bold enough to press the gates.
quest.village-quest.daily.zombie_cull.co: The moaning has faded from the hedgerows, and the road feels safer again.
A village sleeps easier when someone thins the dead before dusk.
Take your pay for the cull.
quest.village-quest.daily.skeleton_patro: Too many skeletons have been sighted near the fields and watchpaths.
Bring down 6 skeletons so the farmers and guards can move without arrows in their backs.
The paths are quieter now that fewer bows answer the dark.
Anyone who breaks a patrol of bones earns honest gratitude.
Here is your reward for the watch.
quest.village-quest.daily.spider_sweep.o: Fresh webs keep appearing around barns, fences, and the low orchard walls.
Kill 6 spiders before the whole yard turns into a nest of silk and eyes.
quest.village-quest.daily.spider_sweep.c: The webs are breaking, and the yard finally feels clear again.
You spared the villagers another night of creeping shadows.
Take these coins for your trouble.
One careless creeper can ruin more work than a day can rebuild.
Hunt down 4 creepers before they tear open the paths, walls, or storehouses.
The walls still stand, and the village keeps its hard-earned shape for another day.
Stopping a blast before it happens is worth more than patching after.
quest.village-quest.special.apiarist_smo: Our hives are thriving, but good tending needs more than one careful pair of hands.
Harvest 8 honey bottles and 4 honeycomb cuts from full hives, and I will entrust you with my Apiarist's Smoker.
You worked the hives with a patient hand, and the whole apiary settled around you.
Take this smoker. Use it well, and the bees will answer you in kind.
message.village-quest.special.apiarist_s: The smoker has already spent all 10 uses for today.
The smoker readies the hive for harvest, settles the nearby bees, and has %1$s/%2$s uses left today.
The smoker settles the nearby bees and has %1$s/%2$s uses left today.
The apiary work is complete. Return to the Questmaster and claim the smoker.
quest.village-quest.special.surveyor_com: A true wayfinder needs sharp eyes, good ore underfoot, and a route home when the world turns dark.

Gather 64 redstone dust from your own mining, craft a Netherite Pickaxe after accepting this task, and turn in one plain unenchanted Netherite Pickaxe made for this work. The ledger will not accept old village stock from a chest. In return, you will receive the Wayfinder's Compass and 3 Netherite Ingots.
You have proven both patience underground and the discipline to prepare for the long road back.
Take the Wayfinder's Compass and these Netherite Ingots. Let them carry you far and still bring you home.
text.village-quest.special.surveyor_comp: 1. I will chart my way home
message.village-quest.special.surveyor_c: Bring one plain unenchanted Netherite Pickaxe for the turn-in.
The wayfinding work is complete. Return to the Questmaster with your plain Netherite Pickaxe.
Wayfinder's Compass now points toward %1$s (%2$s blocks).
No home point could be found.
The Wayfinder's Compass needs %s before it can return you home again.
The Wayfinder leans toward home. Right-click it again within %s to return.
The Wayfinder's Compass returns you home.
The Wayfinder takes the bearing of %s.
This land can be impressed into the Wayfinder here. Right-click it to inscribe %s.
Old roadmarks answer here. Right-click the Wayfinder to inscribe %s.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.special: Harvest from full hives and return for the smoker.
The hive-work is done. Claim the Apiarist's Smoker.
Requires 200 Farming reputation.
Farming reputation required: %s/%s
Finish the wayfinding work and bring a plain Netherite Pickaxe.
The route home is prepared. Claim the Wayfinder's Compass.
Requires 200 Crafting reputation.
Crafting reputation required: %s/%s
The Wayfinder still needs the Apiary Charter before it can be steadied against the land.
Take the Wayfinder to the four field bearings and return once each has been inscribed.
The bearings are set. Bring the Wayfinder back to the Questmaster.
Requires the Apiary Charter and the Wayfinder's Compass.
Readies up to 10 hives per day and calms nearby bees.
Begins with Home. Later, the Questmaster can steady it against field bearings, and a pilgrim can add old roadmarks.
Unlock: the Wayfinder can now read the wider land, from plains and forest to peaks, coast, and mushroom fields.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.story.u: No story chapter is ready to be shown right now.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.story.l: Finish the currently active story chapter before taking another.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.story.f: The fields recovered, the market steadied, and the Apiary Charter now stands with the village.
Project effect: future Farming reputation rewards gain +5, and the Questmaster can now help steady the Wayfinder against the land.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.special: %3$s reputation: %1$s/%2$s
screen.village-quest.journal.projects.st: Some improvements belong to the village itself. Once built, their effects stay with your character.
message.village-quest.questmaster.story_: The Questmaster opens a new page in his ledger. From now on, he will also trust you with larger village stories.
message.village-quest.questmaster.specia: Your name now carries weight in the village. As your standing grows, the Questmaster will entrust you with rarer commissions.
message.village-quest.questmaster.shard_: The Magic Shards hum with a restless pull. Perhaps the Questmaster knows what they are trying to become.
message.village-quest.story.ready: Return to the Questmaster. %s is now ready to turn in.
message.village-quest.special.surveyor_c: The Apiary Charter gives the Questmaster enough sure landmarks to steady your Wayfinder. Ask him about field bearings.
The field bearings are set. Return to the Questmaster with the Wayfinder.
The Wayfinder now reads the wider lay of the land: field, forest, height, shore, and rarer ground alike.
The Wayfinder now answers a fuller chain of roadmarks, from village signs to deeper ruins and older halls.
command.village-quest.questadmin.story.r: No story state was present to reset.
An admin reset your story progress.
command.village-quest.questadmin.story.c: No active story chapter could be completed.
Completed the active story chapter for %s.
An admin completed your active story chapter.
command.village-quest.questadmin.project: Unknown or fixed project. Use a valid unlockable project id.
That project was already in the requested state.
quest.village-quest.project.village_ledg: The Questmaster now keeps your standing, active arcs, and long-term village work in one ledger.
Effect: story arcs and village projects are recorded and surfaced in the journal.
Since it was entered, the village treats your work as something meant to last, not merely something finished.
quest.village-quest.project.apiary_chart: The village formally recognizes your work among the hives and fields.
Effect: future Farming reputation rewards gain +5, and the Questmaster can now help steady the Wayfinder against the land.
quest.village-quest.special.surveyor_com: A wayfinder that knows only home is safe, but not yet steady. To read the land itself, it must be balanced against places that never mistake themselves.
Carry the Wayfinder to a desert, a jungle, frozen peaks, and mushroom fields. When each place rings true, right-click the compass there to inscribe it, then bring it back.
The Questmaster turns the ring slowly in the light and says it no longer wavers between field and horizon.
From now on, the Wayfinder can read a far wider spread of open country, shore, forest, height, and rarer ground alike.
quest.village-quest.project.apiary_chart: Since it was sealed, field work and hive work are spoken of as one careful duty.
quest.village-quest.story.failing_harves: The Failing Harvest
Thin Fields
The wheat came in too sparse, and the root baskets look no better. If the village is to stay calm, the first answer must come from the fields.
Harvest 16 ripe wheat crops and 8 ripe potato plants so the storehouse can begin to breathe again.
Wheat %s/%s | Potatoes %s/%s
Fresh grain and roots finally return to the ledger in honest numbers.
It is not abundance yet, but it is enough to quiet the first worry.
The Questmaster marks the first chapter of the harvest crisis as answered.
Quiet Hives
The hives should have carried some of the burden, yet even the apiary has gone strangely quiet.
Collect 3 honey bottles and 1 honeycomb cut from full hives so the village can judge whether the bees still answer the season.
Honey Bottles %s/%s | Honeycomb Cuts %s/%s
The apiary answers again, slow but steady, and the mood around the yard begins to soften.
A quiet hive says more about a season than most villagers ever hear.
The Questmaster now has proof that the bees have not abandoned the village.
Bread For The Square
Fields and hives may promise relief, but people believe only when the square smells like food again.
Craft 6 bread and pull 4 baked potatoes from the oven after taking this chapter, so the market can see warm proof instead of anxious rumor.
Bread %s/%s | Baked Potatoes %s/%s
Warm bread returns to the square, and even the worried voices grow less sharp around the stalls.
A village believes its future a little more once the ovens speak for it.
The Questmaster marks the market mood as stabilized for the day.
Market Relief
One last proof remains: if trade still moves, the village can recover. If the stalls stay dead, fear will win before hunger does.
Complete 2 villager trades and earn 4 emeralds from them to show that the village economy still answers steady hands.
Trades %s/%s | Emeralds %s/%s
The market answers, the coin still moves, and the harvest scare loosens its grip on the village.
Your work has earned more than thanks. It has earned trust and a place in the village's long plans.
The Apiary Charter is now entered into the village ledger in your name.
Not every good day of trade begins with a crate. Sometimes it begins with someone walking the stalls, keeping faces talking to each other again.
Speak with 3 employed villagers and complete 1 real villager trade so the market starts moving again.
The stalls feel more open the moment somebody is visibly tending to them.
A healthy market runs on trust between the people behind the goods, not only on the goods themselves.
Take wages for your market round.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.story.s: The forge burns cleanly again, the hall is provisioned, and the Forge Charter now carries your name.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.story.m: The road speaks with the village again, the stalls are awake, and the Market Charter now stands as your work.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.story.r: The pens stand in order again, the pasture voices have settled, and the Pasture Charter now belongs to the village.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.story.n: The night watch carries weight again, the village has seen a fresh victory, and the Watch Bell now stands in your name.
screen.village-quest.questmaster.story.a: %1$s of %2$s village stories have been carried through to completion.
Project effect: future Crafting rewards grant +2 levels.
Project effect: once the Wayfinder has its field bearings, the pilgrim can lay old roadmarks upon it.
Project effect: future Animals rewards grant +2 silvermarks.
Project effect: future Roadside Watch reputation rewards gain +5, and the pilgrim may bring one dangerous road rumor each day.
message.village-quest.story.market_road_: By evening, prices are being called again and shutters are lifting. The market lives. Return to the Questmaster.
quest.village-quest.project.forge_charte: The village hall now recognizes your hand as the one that held furnace, bench, and steel back together.
Effect: future Crafting rewards grant +2 levels.
Since it was hung in the hall, good ironwork is measured against the working standard you restored there.
quest.village-quest.project.market_chart: The village trade flow now bears your name, because you brought stalls, ledgers, and trust back to life together.
Effect: once the Wayfinder has its field bearings, the pilgrim can lay old roadmarks upon it.
Since it was written, the market road carries more order, and even the pilgrim now treats the village as a place whose signs are worth reading.
quest.village-quest.project.pasture_char: The village pastures and pens are counted orderly again, now that you brought feed, wool, and a steady hand back into one line of work.
Effect: future Animals rewards grant +2 silvermarks.
Since it was entered, the pens are spoken of as tended ground again, and good stock work leaves visible value behind.
quest.village-quest.project.watch_bell.d: The village night watch has been reset around your work, and every alarm now carries more weight.
quest.village-quest.project.watch_bell.e: Effect: future Roadside Watch reputation rewards gain +5, and the pilgrim may bring one dangerous road rumor each day.
quest.village-quest.project.watch_bell.m: Since it was raised, the night watch sounds like a duty people trust, and word now reaches you sooner when the road edge turns dangerous.
quest.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.roa: The pilgrim says your Wayfinder reads the land now, but old roadmarks are something else. They must be taken where road and memory have stood together long enough to leave a mark.
Carry the Wayfinder to a village, a pillager outpost, a woodland mansion, and a swamp hut. At each place, right-click it once to take the mark, then bring it back.
The pilgrim studies the gathered marks on the ring, deepens them with a careful thumb, and hands the Wayfinder back.
From now on, the old roadmarks answer as plainly as the land itself.
Roadmarks for the Compass is settled.
screen.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.re: Unlock: the Wayfinder can now answer a fuller chain of roadmarks, from villages and outposts to deeper ruins and older halls.
quest.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.que: The pilgrim says the verge above the hill road has gone dark again. Skeletal shapes are lingering there too long for anyone to relight the lanterns with a steady hand.
Clear 6 skeletons from that rise by night so the lanterns can be lit and trusted again by morning.
By the time the pilgrim leaves, fresh lantern light has returned to the verge and nobody is hurrying past that stretch anymore.
A watched road stays open longer than a merely repaired one.
Lanterns for the Verge is settled.
quest.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.woo: The pilgrim says the camp beyond the milestone has been sleeping badly. Creepers keep rolling in close enough to snuff the fire whenever the carts settle.
Clear 3 creepers from that camp edge by night so the next fire can hold till dawn.
When the pilgrim moves on, people are already speaking of the milestone camp as a place where a fire can burn through the night again.
A safe camp buys a whole road a little courage.
Smoke Beyond the Milestone is settled.
quest.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.tra: The pilgrim says the lower road has been carrying the sound of shuffling feet after moonrise. Nothing grand, just enough dead weight to make late carts take the longer bend.
Clear 8 zombies from the lower roadside by night so the track stops feeling claimed by the dark.
By the next pass, the ruts look like cart tracks again instead of something that ought to be watched from afar.
A road is often lost in the feet that linger on it before it is lost in stone.
Tracks in the Dark is settled.
quest.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.fan: The pilgrim says the hedgerow along the field road has gone web-thick again. Too many eyes are sitting low there for anyone to trust the edge after dusk.
Clear 6 spiders from that hedgerow by night so the field road feels walked rather than watched.
The hedgerow loosens, and the field road stops feeling like a place that belongs to waiting eyes.
Sometimes clearing a road means nothing more heroic than giving it back its edge.
Fangs by the Hedgerow is settled.
quest.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.ash: The pilgrim says one of the ash passes beyond the village's knowing has gone ugly again. Blaze-fire hangs too close to the way, and black-boned wardens have begun to linger where no honest traveler should have to pass them.
Clear 4 blazes and 3 wither skeletons from that Nether pass so it can be crossed without paying for every step in blood and cinders.
When the pilgrim speaks of the pass again, it is as a hard road made passable, not a death-trap left to its own temper.
Far roads do not stay open by luck. They stay open because someone chooses to pay the harder kind of price first.
Ash on the Pass is settled.
quest.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.smo: The pilgrim says a blackstone stretch farther down the Nether way has gone sour. Magma things are rolling where the footing narrows, and ghast-fire is making the open crossings too costly to chance.
Clear 5 magma cubes and 2 ghasts from that blackstone route so the smoke above it belongs to travel again rather than panic.
The blackstone route is spoken of again as a hard crossing, not a foolish one.
Some roads only stay in memory because someone keeps them from becoming a warning instead.
quest.village-quest.pilgrim.contract.sti: The pilgrim speaks more quietly of the gate-road than of any other. Beyond it, the air carries a silence that should not be trusted, and too many watching shapes have begun to gather around the old route again.
Clear 6 endermen and 3 shulkers from that far road so the gate beyond the village can be spoken of as reachable, not merely feared.
When the pilgrim next names that road, it sounds less like a warning and more like a distance that can be borne.
The farthest roads are never made friendly. Only survivable, and only by careful hands.
Stillness beyond the Gate is settled.
quest.village-quest.story.silent_forge.c: Cold Hearth
The forge is not only quiet, it is hollow. Without ore, coal, and a few rarer finds, nobody will believe this hall is ready to make honest work again.
Mine 18 iron ore, 12 coal ore, and 5 diamond ore so the Questmaster can mark real fire for the hall again.
Iron Ore %s/%s | Coal Ore %s/%s
Diamond Ore %s/%s
For the first time in days, the forge sounds like material instead of rumor.
Iron, coal, and rare ore say more to the hall than promises ever could.
The Questmaster draws the first line beneath the reopening of the forge.
The Bellows Breathe Again
A hall only becomes a forge again when it has more than heat. It needs order: a proper furnace mouth for the work and a quench kettle to harden the metal.
Pull 24 iron ingots from the furnace, then set aside a Blast Furnace and a Cauldron so the Questmaster can enter the hall back into the ledger as a proper smithy.
Iron Ingots %s/%s
Blast Furnace %s/%s | Cauldron %s/%s
Fire, draft, and quench water now stand where they belong.
The hall has its backbone again, and only then can good steel follow.
The Questmaster writes the forge back into the ledger as working space.
Tools For The Hall
An open furnace without tools is only hot air. The hall needs pieces that prove useful work can come out of it again.

Put together a small working kit for the reopened hall: an iron pickaxe, a bucket, shears, and a shield. The Questmaster wants fresh forgework from your own hand, not old village stock taken from a chest.
Pickaxe %s/%s | Bucket %s/%s
Shears %s/%s | Shield %s/%s
The hall now holds tools that look deliberate rather than scavenged.
Each piece says something different about the return of careful workmanship.
The Questmaster marks the forge as able to supply the village again.
The Master's Edge
Fire and tools are not enough. The village must see a blade that proves masterwork is possible here again.

Buy a Sharpness book of any level from a villager, bind it to a fresh Diamond Sword on an anvil, and bring that finished blade to the Questmaster. Old village steel from a chest will not satisfy the ledger here.
Sharpness Book Bought %s/%s
Diamond Sword Sharpened %s/%s
Finished Blade Carried %s/%s
The hall has produced a blade again that feels worthy, not merely sharp.
From now on, people will not speak of the old forge. They will speak of the one you restored.
The Forge Charter is now entered into the village ledger in your name.
quest.village-quest.story.market_road_tr: Market Road Troubles
Shuttered Stalls
The market road does not first suffer from empty shelves. It suffers from missing coin. Until emeralds move again, every rumor weighs more than every crate.
Earn 32 emeralds from real villager trades so the first stalls have reason to throw their shutters open again.
Emeralds %s/%s
Coin is visibly moving from hand to hand again.
Where emeralds flow honestly, certain fears go quiet faster than promises ever could.
The Questmaster notes that the market road has answered for the first time in days.
Ledger And Notices
Trade does not live on crates and voices alone. It needs paper: notices, prices, debts, and agreements. Without writing, even a good market runs short of breath.
Bring 32 Paper and 3 Books to the Questmaster so the market road can work with ledgers, notices, and proper order again.
Paper %s/%s | Books %s/%s
Paper and books return to the market, and with them comes accountability.
A village trusts a price more easily once it can be written down.
The Questmaster sets the market ledgers in order once more.
Goods Must Flow
Order alone does not open a stall. People must see goods truly moving up and down the road again.
Complete 10 real villager trades so it becomes plain that the market road does not only promise, but delivers again.
Trades %s/%s
Crates, doors, and hands now work to the same rhythm again.
A market begins to heal only when the flow of goods starts to feel natural again.
The Questmaster records that the road is moving properly again.
Market Day Returns
The last step is neither cart nor ledger, but face and word. Go stall by stall, listen well, and let the people feel that someone is holding the market together again.
Make the rounds of 5 staffed stalls and workshops, so market day returns as shared village work instead of scattered private worry.
Staffed Stalls Visited %s/%s
In the end, it is not only trade that has returned, but mood.
The market road now carries voices, prices, and confidence at the same time again.
The Market Charter is now entered into the village ledger in your name.
Restless Pens
Empty Troughs
The pens do not first suffer from missing animals, but from failing order. When feed is not laid in and no new life breaks the silence, people soon begin to speak of neglected care.
Set 4 breedings in motion and keep 2 hay bales ready, so the pastures plainly show work again.
Breeding Pairs %s/%s
Hay Bales %s/%s
There is movement at the pens again, and even the skeptical looks at the fence begin to soften.
A stable feels healthier the moment feed and young stock can be seen together again.
The Questmaster notes that the pastures look ordered for the first time in days.
Wool Before Weather
When the wind turns, a village learns quickly whether its pastures merely live or truly prepare. Before the weather breaks, there must again be enough fleece and store laid by.
Shear 4 sheep and bring 12 wool of any color to the Questmaster, so loom-work and winter stores no longer pull against each other.
Sheared Sheep %s/%s
Wool %s/%s
The wool bundles rise again, and the pasture no longer looks like work left undone.
Wool steadies a village long before the first cold evening truly arrives.
The Questmaster lowers the worry around the pens by a clear measure.
Words At The Pens
Yet troughs and bundles are not enough. If the pens are to hold again, the hands at field, fence, butcher's block, and leather bench must begin speaking with one another once more.
Make the rounds among 3 of the hands tied to the pens, so common measure returns there in place of quiet grumbling.
Hands heard %s/%s
Work around the pens sounds coordinated again instead of frayed.
A stable often begins to heal in conversation before it heals at the fence.
The Questmaster records that the unease around the pastures is easing.
The Shepherd's Call
The last step is not another cut, but a sign. When bell and herd answer each other again, the village knows the pens are not merely supplied, but gathered back into order.
Ring a bell while at least 6 adult pasture animals stand gathered nearby, so the shepherd's call returns to the village as a shared sign once more.
Calls Gathered %s/%s
The bell-strike now meets an answer instead of scattered motion.
From this day on, the pens are counted not as a worry, but as part of the village's order again.
The Pasture Charter is now entered into the village ledger in your name.
message.village-quest.story.restless_pen: The voices around the pens are speaking with each other again instead of against each other. Return to the Questmaster.
Too few animals answer the call. Only %1$s of the needed %2$s pasture animals are gathered nearby.
quest.village-quest.story.night_bells.ch: First Watch
People sleep only when the first watch is more than form. Tonight it must become plain that someone is truly clearing the ground around the village.
Clear 6 zombies from the first watch at night so the houses nearest the road can rest again.
First watch cleared %s/%s
The first shadows fall back, and the heavy unease around the houses falls with them.
A good first watch tells the village that the night does not rule it.
The Questmaster marks the first night as properly held again.
Thin The Dark
If skeletons hold the lanes and spiders take the outer fields, nobody will keep faith in safe paths between house, field, and pen.
Drive 4 skeletons and 4 spiders off the village edge at night so the ways between house, field, and pen feel usable again by morning.
Road edge %s/%s skeletons | %s/%s spiders
The road belongs to the village again, not the shapes that wait at its edge.
Fewer shadows mean more courage for everyone who must step out first in the morning.
The Questmaster marks the second watch-line as secured.
Hold The Road
Some nights do not test a village with a single foe, but by whether the whole edge still holds when everything presses at once.
Hold the road through a hard night: stop 2 creepers and beat back 10 hostile creatures in all, so the verge is kept rather than merely passed through.
Road held %s/%s creepers | %s/%s hostiles
The night still tests the village, but it no longer slips through unopposed.
Whoever holds the road-edge often holds together the courage of the whole village.
The Questmaster records that the watch carries weight again.
Morning After The Alarm
The true proof of a watch is not a single swing, but whether the village can stand through a whole assault and still greet morning together.
Successfully complete a raid and then return to the Questmaster so the night watch is counted not only as brave, but as proven.
Successful Raids %s/%s
The village still carries the blessing of an older victory. Let it fade, then lead a fresh raid defense.
Morning does not arrive in exhausted silence, but in loud relief.
A village that survives a raid remembers for a long time who stood at its bell.
The Watch Bell is now entered into the village ledger in your name.
quest.village-quest.special.admin_core_t: Core systems test complete. Use /questadmin coretest reset to run it again.
message.village-quest.special.admin_core: The admin core systems test is now active.
Core systems test completed. Use /questadmin coretest reset to run it again.
command.village-quest.questadmin.coretes: Started the admin core systems test for %s.
Reset the admin core systems test for %s.
No admin core systems test is active for %s.
